featureName: Sprint 31-37 — Bug Fixes, Validation & UX Hardening (6-hour cycle)
description: As both a Client and Provider using NeighbourGo daily, fix real bugs (broken delete-account flow, exposed dev login), harden form validation, add error-retry states, improve search UX, and let providers manage their own service listings.

userStories.title: Fix Delete Account — Require Typing DELETE to Confirm
userStories.description: The delete-account dialog says 'Type DELETE to confirm' but doesn't actually require the user to type anything — a single tap deletes the account. Add a real TextField that validates the input before enabling the delete button.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete account dialog contains a TextField with hint 'Type DELETE to confirm'
Delete button is disabled (greyed out) until TextField value equals 'DELETE' (case-sensitive)
Pressing delete with correct input shows a loading spinner, then signs out on success
Pressing delete with incorrect input does nothing (button stays disabled)
Cancel button closes the dialog without side effects
flutter analyze — 0 errors

userStories.title: Hide Dev Login Button in Release Builds
userStories.description: The welcome screen exposes a 'Dev Login (Skip Auth)' button that bypasses authentication. This must only be visible in debug/profile mode, never in release builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Dev Login button is wrapped in a kDebugMode or kReleaseMode check
In debug mode: button appears and functions as before
In release mode: button is completely absent from the widget tree (not just hidden)
No other auth bypass paths exist in welcome_screen.dart
flutter analyze — 0 errors

userStories.title: Error States with Retry on Key Screens
userStories.description: TaskDetailScreen, PublicProfileScreen, ServiceDetailScreen, and ChatThreadScreen show generic 'Error: $e' text with no way to recover. Add a consistent error widget with a Retry button that re-fetches the data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or reuse an ErrorRetryWidget(message, onRetry) in core/widgets/
TaskDetailScreen error state shows ErrorRetryWidget; tapping Retry calls ref.invalidate on the task provider
PublicProfileScreen error state shows ErrorRetryWidget; tapping Retry re-fetches the profile
ServiceDetailScreen error state shows ErrorRetryWidget; tapping Retry re-fetches the listing
ChatThreadScreen error state shows ErrorRetryWidget; tapping Retry re-fetches messages
Error message is user-friendly (e.g. 'Something went wrong') not raw exception text
flutter analyze — 0 errors

userStories.title: Discover Search — Loading Indicator, No-Results State, Result Count
userStories.description: When searching in Discover, there's no feedback during the debounce/loading period, no message when no results match, and no indication of how many results were found.
acceptanceCriteria: While search query is debouncing or loading, show a subtle LinearProgressIndicator below the search bar
When search returns 0 results, show an empty-state illustration with 'No results for "query"' and a suggestion to adjust filters
When search returns results, show a count label (e.g. '12 tasks found') between search bar and list
Clearing the search field resets to the default unfiltered view
Works for all Discover tabs (Tasks, Providers, Services)
flutter analyze — 0 errors

userStories.title: Service Listing Management — Edit and Delete Own Listings
userStories.description: Providers can create service listings but cannot edit or delete them afterwards. Add edit and delete capabilities accessible from ServiceDetailScreen when the viewer is the listing owner.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceDetailScreen shows Edit (pencil) and Delete (trash) icons in the AppBar when currentUser is the listing owner
Edit navigates to CreateServiceScreen pre-populated with existing listing data (title, description, category, pricing, photos)
Saving edits updates the Firestore document and shows a success snackbar
Delete shows a confirmation dialog; confirming deletes the document and navigates back
Non-owners see no edit/delete buttons
flutter analyze — 0 errors

userStories.title: Form Validation Hardening — Email, Bids, Budget, Profile Rates
userStories.description: Multiple forms accept invalid input: email validation is too loose, bid amounts have no upper bound warning, task budget has no min/max guidance, and provider rates accept non-numeric values.
acceptanceCriteria: Email auth uses a proper regex that rejects addresses without valid domain (e.g. 'user@' or 'user@.com')
Submit bid sheet shows a warning chip when bid exceeds 2x the task budget (still allows submission)
Post task budget step enforces minimum S$5 and shows helper text 'Min S$5'
EditProfileScreen rate fields only accept numeric input (InputFormatter) and enforce S$1–S$500 range
All validation errors show inline red helper text below the field
flutter analyze — 0 errors

userStories.title: Actionable Empty States — Chat List, My Tasks, My Bids
userStories.description: Empty screens show minimal text with no clear call to action. Add contextual empty-state illustrations and CTA buttons that guide users to the logical next step.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatListScreen empty state: illustration + 'No conversations yet' + 'Browse Tasks' button (navigates to Discover)
MyTasksScreen empty state (provider): illustration + 'No assigned tasks' + 'Find Tasks' button
MyBidsScreen empty state: illustration + 'No bids yet' + 'Browse Open Tasks' button
PosterHomeScreen empty state: illustration + 'Post your first task' + 'Post a Task' button
Each empty state uses a Lottie animation or SVG illustration consistent with app design
flutter analyze — 0 errors
